updated_conclusion: The worker registry path behind 0x14066DC10 now has a concrete sorted-vector model. The parent object owns a 0x18-byte record vector at +0x70 keyed by the worker payload dword at +0x20. 0x14066E300 is a no-pdata lower_bound helper, 0x14066DD60 erases and compacts records, 0x14066F140 plus its split .pdata tail inserts/reallocates records, and 0x14066E560 wraps lookup-or-insert with ref-counted pair handoff and release behavior.

record_model.division_evidence: Helpers repeatedly use magic multiplier 0x2AAAAAAAAAAAAAAB plus shifts to divide byte spans by 0x18.

lower_bound_helper_0x14066e300.pdata_status: No containing .pdata runtime-function entry; the previous .pdata range ends exactly at 0x14066E300.
observed_behavior: Reads vector begin/end from RCX and requested key from RDX.
Returns the first record whose key is not below the requested key, or the vector end pointer.
lower_bound_helper_0x14066e300.confidence: high for control flow, key offset, and return pointer shape

erase_helper_0x14066dd60.pdata_note: The body crosses small adjacent .pdata fragments, so the full helper was scanned as an explicit range.
erase_helper_0x14066dd60.direct_call_context: 0x14066D7B4 calls it after using 0x14066E300 to find a matching worker-key record under the parent +0x70 vector.

ordered_calls.role: Copies or moves one 0x18-byte record during compaction.
Destroys or clears the old final record after end is decremented.

observed_behavior: Receives RCX as the vector header and RDX as the record to erase.
If records exist after the erased record, repeatedly moves following records left by 0x18 bytes through 0x14034FF80.
Subtracts 0x18 from [RCX+8], calls the final-record cleanup helper, and returns the erased record pointer.

insert_helper_0x14066f140.pdata_note: The logical helper is split across consecutive .pdata entries at 0x14066F17A and 0x14066F24B. The 0x14066F24B tail is the reallocation path, not a separate semantic operation.

ordered_calls.role: Moves 0x18-byte records during in-place gap creation.
Releases a displaced ref-counted object when its strong count drops to zero.
Allocates a larger 0x18-byte record buffer for the reallocation path.
Runs cleanup over the old begin/end record range before buffer release.

ordered_calls.target: CALL R10 from global allocator/manager state at 0x140E57B40
ordered_calls.role: Releases the old record buffer after migration.

observed_behavior: Receives RCX as vector header, RDX as insertion position, and R8 as a temporary record.
Steals the temp record key and two qwords, then clears the temp pair fields to transfer ownership.
When capacity remains, duplicates the last record into the spare end slot, shifts records down by 0x18, writes the stolen record into the insertion position, and advances [RCX+8] by 0x18.

When full, allocates a larger buffer, copies the prefix, places the stolen record, migrates the suffix, clears moved-from pair fields, destroys the old range, releases the old buffer, and updates begin/end/capacity.
insert_helper_0x14066f140.role_name: sorted record insert with in-place and reallocation paths
insert_helper_0x14066f140.confidence: high for record movement and ownership transfer; medium for allocator-manager identity

ordered_calls.role: Delegates insertion when the missing-key path cannot be satisfied by a simple append.
Releases the stolen temp pair when insertion does not consume it.

observed_behavior: Reads the candidate key and two-qword pair from R8, then clears R8+8/R8+0x10 so ownership is controlled by the helper.
Binary-searches the vector by dword key and returns the existing slot when the key is already present.
On a missing key, appends directly when possible or delegates to 0x14066F140, then recomputes the result slot after possible reallocation.
Writes an out-parameter pair through RDX: slot pointer at [RDX] and an inserted/existing flag at [RDX+8].
Iterates 0x10-byte active entries from parent +0xE0 to +0xE8.
Clears per-entry payload fields at +0x28/+0x30 and releases any previous ref-counted payload.
Calls a virtual method at entry-object vtable +0x20, locks parent +0x20 through 0x1407B5200, and lower_bound searches the parent +0x70 sorted vector using the entry object's dword at +0x20.
When a matching keyed record is present, calls 0x14066DD60 to erase it, then unlocks through 0x1407B5260.
After the loop, calls 0x1400310A0 and resets [parent+0xE8] to [parent+0xE0].
cleanup_drain_0x14066d7b4.confidence: medium-high for container and erase behavior; medium for exact lifecycle phase

relationship_to_previous_milestones.worker-validation-container-and-bootstra: Completes the helper names behind 0x14066DDF0 and explains how returned record+0x8 slots remain stable across insertion and reallocation.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.worker-name-registration-helper: Grounds 0x14066DC10's worker-record publication in a concrete sorted id-to-pair registry model.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.js5-worker-provider-cluster-lifecycle: The Js5WorkerThread path now has a named registry container and cleanup mechanism shared with MixerWorker and VorbisWorker.

tooling_observation.remaining_tool_gap: Full instruction-window dumps still require GhidraDumpWindowsPy. A future native pe-instructions or pe-disasm-window command would make the reallocation/erase helper workflow fully repeatable inside Reverser.

next_targets: Trace 0x14066F3F0 and 0x1407CD29C to name the callback/task wrapper installed by 0x14066E030.
Trace 0x14066D010, 0x1400D24B0, and 0x1400D1990 to connect constructor-side worker labels to concrete provider object lifetimes.
Promote a native instruction-window disassembler into Reverser so Ghidra is not required for short helper-body dumps.
